  3. The Technical Committee

The Technical Committee

This is a plain-English guide. The official IPF Technical Rules are the final word.

The Technical Committee is the IPF body in charge of all technical matters: the rules, the referees and the equipment.

Who is on it

A chairman who is a Category 1 referee, up to ten members from different nations, and a coach or lifter representative.

What it does

  • Reviews technical proposals from member federations
  • Appoints the referees and jury for World Championships
  • Trains and examines referees
  • Runs referee courses before major competitions
  • Publishes technical material on training and the lifts
  • Inspects competition and personal equipment, including items seeking IPF approval

Equipment approval

Makers who want to use "IPF Approved" submit their products for inspection. If an item passes and a fee is paid, a certificate is issued, renewed each year. If a design changes and no longer meets the rules, approval is withdrawn at once.

Next: world and international records
